text.title: rust-testing-unit Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the rust-testing-unit methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Colocate unit tests with production code in #[cfg(test)] mod tests { use super::*; }. Black-box tests go to tests/ at the crate root. Never mix the two placements.
rule.rationale: Colocated tests access private items; tests/ tests verify the public contract. Mixing produces a confusing import surface.

rule.statement: Mock trait dependencies with mockall::mock! or #[automock]. Do NOT mock plain structs or types with cheap real implementations.
rule.rationale: Mocking a concrete type like chrono::DateTime adds noise without coverage gain.

rule.statement: Always specify the tokio runtime flavor explicitly: current_thread for non-spawning tests; multi_thread when the SUT calls spawn/spawn_blocking.
rule.rationale: multi_thread default masks Send/Sync bugs; current_thread default masks spawn_blocking failures.

rule.statement: Use tokio::time::pause to control virtual time. Never use std::thread::sleep or real tokio::time::sleep to wait for tasks.
rule.rationale: sleep-driven waits flake in CI; pause + advance gives deterministic sub-millisecond tests.

rule.statement: Use expect("descriptive message") instead of unwrap() in test assertions.
rule.rationale: unwrap() in a test panics without context; expect documents intent.

rule.statement: Only add doctests to public items. Doctests on private fns are silently skipped by cargo test --doc.
rule.rationale: Silent skip misleads developers and coverage reporters.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
